market_context: Markets opened volatile due to Iran-Trump tensions over Strait of Hormuz, with oil prices surging then plunging after Trump postponed strikes. Futures showed strong reversal from negative to positive on de-escalation hopes.

predictions.reasoning: Strong premarket reversal with S&P futures up 1.95% after Trump postponed Iran strikes for five days. Oil price collapse from $113+ to sub-$100 should boost equities as energy costs fall and recession fears ease.

Tech should outperform on de-escalation relief rally given growth sensitivity to oil prices. Strong technical bounce expected from oversold levels with Nasdaq futures positive after four weeks of conflict pressure.

Dow should benefit most from energy cost relief given industrial/value tilt. Dow futures up over 2% premarket on Iran talks progress. Less volatile than tech, better positioned for oil shock recovery.

Energy sector should plunge on massive oil price collapse from $113+ to sub-$100 after Trump postponed Iran strikes. Brent crude down 14% intraday according to Bloomberg - energy stocks will follow oil prices down sharply.

Fear gauge should drop significantly on Iran de-escalation news and Trump postponing strikes. VIX at elevated 26.78 level from war fears - five-day postponement should allow volatility to compress meaningfully.
